### Introduction

The visual exploration feature i.e EasyNav in our Knowledge Graph tool provides users with the ability to navigate and analyze Knowledge Graphs in a visual manner. This feature allows users to save and share their explorations, as well as add new instances and relationships to the graph. With the EasyNav editor, users can enjoy enhanced editing capabilities, resulting in improved efficiency, accuracy, and data control. This feature aims to enhance data quality and productivity for users.

### Usage

The visual exploration feature in our Knowledge Graph tool can be utilized in various scenarios. Here are a few examples:

- 1. Data Analysis: Users can explore and analyze complex data sets represented as Knowledge Graphs, enabling them to identify patterns, dependencies, and trends. By visually examining the connections between entities, users can gain a holistic understanding of their data, facilitating more informed decision-making.

- 2. Knowledge Discovery: Researchers and domain experts can utilize the visual exploration feature to discover new insights and relationships within their domain of expertise. By navigating through the Knowledge Graph, users can uncover hidden connections, discover emerging trends, and generate novel hypotheses for further investigation.

- 3. Data Management : The EasyNav editor, with its enhanced editing capabilities, empowers users to efficiently manage their data within the Knowledge Graph. Users can add new instances, modify existing data, and ensure data accuracy and integrity. The feature provides a user-friendly interface for data manipulation, simplifying the process of maintaining and updating the Knowledge Graph.

- 4. Save and Share: The ability to save and share explorations enhances collaboration among team members. Users can easily share specific visualizations, findings, and insights with colleagues, promoting knowledge exchange and facilitating collaborative decision-making.

!!! Info

       EasyNav is a module in DataManager in the EXPLORE section. The content of a graph can only be explored in EasyNav if SHACL Shapes have been defined for the Classes and Properties in your graphs. The idea of this is to provide a mechanism to control the amount of information and detail exposed to your end users. At the same time it is a means to present the same graph content differently to certain target groups using bespoke Shape catalogs for each. See https://documentation.eccenca.com/23.1/explore-and-author/graph-exploration/building-a-customized-user-interface/ for more details on how to create SHACL Shapes for your vocabulary concepts (for use in both UI definition and EasyNav).

!!! Note

      In the context of knowledge graphs, SHACL provides a way to define rules and constraints that data should adhere to. It allows you to specify the structure, shape, and integrity constraints of your knowledge graph. This includes defining the types of nodes and properties, cardinality restrictions, value constraints, and relationships between nodes. Cf. https://www.w3.org/TR/shacl/

## Let's jump right into the tutorial and get started

- In Corporate Memory, click **EasyNav** in the navigation under **Explore** on the left side of the page.

- **Select a graph** to explore from the dropdown list.

- **Type** a resource name in the search box and select the same.

!!! Step Result

             Graph opens to explore for visualization.

!!! Info 

       At the welcome screen of the module, users have the option to either load a previously saved visualization; it provides users with the convenience of easily accessing and revisiting previously explored knowledge graphs, saving time and effort.

- Click on **Load visualization** from the catalog in the center. 

- Click on eye icon to **Open visualization** on the right side of the page.
                  
!!! Step Result

              The save visualization is opened.

## Let's dive into the deep to understand the EasyNav features and to explore the graph for visualization.

## Features of the EasyNav

- The new feature shows the count of instances and classes of the graph.

- It provide the option to switch on or off for the show search sidebar.

- We can open a saved visualization from load visualization option on the right side of the page with the  icon.

- It provides the feature to start a new visualization on the right side of the page which allows the user to go back to the main page.

- It provides the feature for undo redo, this functionality allows users to easily revert or reapply changes, enhancing productivity and providing flexibility in navigating.

- It provides a feature to highlight the relationship between all the nodes when we click on the particular node the blue lines indicates the direct relationships of the selected node.

## Exploring a graph 

- Click on graph CMEM Shapes Catalog option and drag to the canvas.

- Click on the dot on the right side of the option CMEM Shapes Catalog to explore.

- Click on the Used properties options it opens the Linked resources box then drag the Linked resources options to the canvas.

!!! Step Result

              The Linked resources i.e nodes/relationship are added to the canvas.

- Click on nodes then click on properties and drag the properties to the canvas.

- Each node and relationship has the properties and sub properties click on the dot on the right side of the node and relationship  and drag on the canvas to explore in detail.

- Click on Save to save the visualization.

- We have feature to add the new instance in the graph visualization 
Drag the Add new instance on the canvas

- Select a node type as SHACL property group.

- Type the Naming and Presentations i.e Label, Description and order then click on Save.

- Click on Save.

- Click on Create.

!!! Step Result

              The new instance has been created


 - Click on the dot of the right side the CMEM shapes catalog and drag to the new instance right dot to connect then select the edge type as label as shown below.

 - Click on Save.

!!! Step Result

              The new instance got connected
